title: Your First 48 Hours: The Checklist
summary: What to photograph, meter, and fix before you unpack so your rental protects you later.

sections.title: The Moment
sections.value: Moving-in afternoon. The landlord has just left. You are standing in a flat full of boxes with no clear order for what to check first.

sections.title: The Feeling
sections.value: Relief from the keys hits at the same time as formless overwhelm. Nobody handed you a manual for the first two days.

sections.title: The Reveal
sections.value: Your first 48 hours as a renter are the easiest time to document problems. What you record now is evidence if something is disputed later.

sections.title: How It Actually Works
sections.value: Walk the condition report with photos and notes. Record meter readings. Test smoke alarms and list defects. Know the return deadline for the report. Change Wi-Fi credentials if included. Keep one folder of photos on your phone.

sections.title: The Bridge
sections.value: When you are ready, find locksmiths, hardware and support services near you.

sections.title: Next Chapter
sections.value: Next: When You Don't Know Anyone Yet — for when the quiet feels louder than the boxes.
